Standard Fees
•	The APS recommended fee for a 46–60 minute session in 2025–2026 is $318. 
•	The AAPi recommended fee for a 46-60 minute session in 2025-2026 is $330. 
•	To balance professional sustainability with client accessibility, Kara offers a generously reduced fee of $250 for a 60-minute session. 
•	Discounted rates apply for low-income earners. 

Medicare Rebates
•	With a Mental Health Treatment Plan (MHTP) from your GP, you are eligible for a Medicare rebate of $145.25 per session with a clinical psychologist.
•	Medicare rebates can be claimed for both in-person and telehealth sessions.
•	Rebates apply to up to 10 sessions per calendar year (under the Better Access initiative).
description:
Clinical services and Forensic services
Kara brings over two decades of experience supporting individuals, couples and communities through evidence-based, trauma-informed psychological care. 

Clinical services (16+):
Assessment and intervention for:
- Anxiety, depression & mood disorders
- Trauma & PTSD (including complex trauma)
- Grief, loss and life transitions
- Relationship and attachment difficulties
- Stress, burnout and workplace challenges
- Neurodiversity-affirming support (ADHD, ASD)
- Personality vulnerabilities and emotional dysregulation
- Health-related and psychosocial stressors. 
Clinical work is grounded in evidence-based modalities, including: CBT, DBT skills, SFBT, EMDR, mindfulness-based interventions and positive psychology. Treatment is tailored, collaborative and trauma-informed. 

Forensic services
Extensive experience across correctional, legal and government systems. Specialised forensic assessment and intervention including:
- Violence and sexual offending risk assessment
- Violent extremism and terrorism assessment and intervention
- Cognitive impairment and decision-making capacity
- Court-informed psychological reports
- Behavioural intervention planning 
- Support for individuals navigating justice, parole or corrective environments
Approach integrates structured professional judgement, evidence-based frameworks and a strong focus on safety, rehabilitation and practicaql pathways for change.
referral_process: Private (fill out contact form on website), GP initiated Mental Health Treatment Plan, NDIS or DVA.

description:
Carer Gateway is an Australian Government program providing free services and support for carers.
Carer Gateway services are free to access for anyone caring for a family member or friend who is living with a disability, a long term medical condition, mental illness, alcohol or drug dependency or someone who is frail due to age.

notes: Big 5 Journal is a personal journal with things to better your mental wellbeing
description:
. Our research team has pinpointed five proven activities that lead to better mental health. And it is now our mission to share this with all Australians so that together, we can feel mentally healthier and more resilient.
Our research has shown that we have a tipping point for good or poor mental health - people who do The Big 5 activities at least 3-4 times each week are likely to have good mental health.
But people who don't do these things very often are likely to have poor mental health. They are more likely to be troubled by symptoms of depression and anxiety, and they are less likely to feel satisfied with their lives.
The Big 5 is a practical approach that can easily fit into our daily lives. The actions are simple and effective, and they don’t require us to make big, daunting changes to our lives. And, most of us are already doing The Big 5, we just might not be doing them enough.

description:
Link2home is the NSW statewide homelessness information and referral telephone service. It is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, every day of the year.
Link2home can provide information, assessments and referrals to homelessness support and accommodation services in NSW everyday from 9am to 10pm.
From 10pm to 9am, Link2home will only provide information and assessments. Referral to accommodation and support services is not possible during these hours. It will refer people to emergency services if needed.
Link2home can help you with:
information about local services
an assessment to determine what kind of help you need (see how Homes NSW uses your personal information)
a referral to an appropriate specialist homelessness service (SHS), support services, temporary accommodation and/or other services.
eligibility: for people who are homeless or who are worried they will become homeless, as well as for advocates acting on a person's behalf.
name: Link2Home

program_name: Multisystemic Therapy for Child Abuse and Neglect
description:
Multisystemic Therapy for Child Abuse and Neglect (MST-CAN) is an Evidenced-Based program that has been specifically designed to support change within vulnerable families who have come to the attention of child protection services.

MST-CAN is a home-based intervention that is delivered at times that are convenient to the family. It is an intensive program, typically 3-4 sessions per week over a period of 6 - 9 months. The team also provide on call support after hours and weekends.   All members of the family are involved in the treatment. Common treatment strategies include safety planning, Cognitive Behavioral Therapies for managing anger and addressing the impact of trauma, Reinforcement-Based Therapy for adult substance misuse, family therapy focused on communication and problem solving.

The goal of MST-CAN is to keep families together, assure the safety of children, prevent abuse and neglect, reduce mental health difficulties experienced by adults and children, and increase families social supports.
The program aims to develop skills within families to cope with the challenges that being a parent can bring, and to build resilience in children.                                                                                                                                              This service delivers a program within the home to help parents learn to play and respond to the developmental needs of their children.
eligibility: Families must have a child aged between 6-17 and had recent cntact with the child protection system for physical abuse or neglect issues
